messages.content: Hi, could you translate this japanes text in English :  2012年9月に発売された『グイン・サーガ・ワールド 5』より、五代ゆう、宵野ゆめによる正編の続編の発表が行われ、2013年11月からは、この続編を文庫化した書籍が正編のナンバリングを受け継ぎ、第131巻「パロの暗黒」（五代ゆう）・132巻「サイロンの挽歌」（宵野ゆめ）として刊行された[9]。以降は随時続巻され、絶筆部分の直接の続きも133巻「魔聖の迷宮」（五代ゆう）より刊行される[10]という、複数作家による巨大なグイン・サーガ・ワールドの様を呈している。本作はかなりの長編作品となっており途中の巻で未読状態になってしまう読者も少なくないが、五代はどの巻から読み始めても前後が分かるように意識して書いていると自身のTwitterでコメントしている[5]。

From "Guin Saga World 5," which was released in September 2012, the announcement of a sequel to the main story by Yuu Kurabayashi and <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> was made. From November 2013 onward, this sequel was published as a paperback version, continuing the numbering of the main story. It was released as the 131st volume, "Paro's Darkness" (Yuu Kurabayashi), and the 132nd volume, "Cyron's Elegy" (Yume Yoino) [9]. Subsequent volumes have been published continuously, and the direct continuation of the final part will be released in the 133rd volume, "Maze of the Demon Saint" (Yuu Kurabayashi) [10]. This shows the grand scale of the Guin Saga World, with multiple authors contributing. This work is quite a long piece, and there are many readers who may have stopped reading in the middle of the volumes. However, Yuu Kurabayashi has commented on her own Twitter that she has consciously written the story so that readers can understand the context regardless of which volume they start reading [5].
2013年〜2016年（131巻〜140巻）の間は奇数巻を五代ゆうが（主にパロ・ヤガ・沿海州の話を担当）、偶数巻を宵野ゆめが（主にケイロニアの話を担当）執筆した。2017年以降（141巻〜）は五代ゆうによる一人体制となっている。

Between 2013 and 2016 (volumes 131-140), Yuu Kurabayashi primarily handled the odd-numbered volumes (mainly focusing on the stories of Paro, Yaga, and the Coastal Province), while Yume Yoino handled the even-numbered volumes (mainly focusing on the story of Keilonia). From 2017 onwards (volume 141-), Yuu Kurabayashi has been the sole author.
synopsis

Unfortunately, without more specific information or context, it is difficult to provide a detailed synopsis of the story. However, based on the given information, "Guin Saga World" is a series of novels that follows a grand narrative with multiple authors. It is set in a world where a sequel to the main story, written by Yuu Kurabayashi and <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, was announced in 2012. The subsequent volumes continue the numbering of the main story and explore various subplots and characters within the larger world. The story is known for its length and complexity, but the author, Yuu Kurabayashi, has stated that readers can start reading from any volume and still understand the overall narrative.
My bad, I was looking for a translation of this word : あらすじ  (I think it might mean synopsis but I'm not sure.)
Yes, you are correct. The word "あらすじ" (arasuji) does mean "synopsis" in Japanese. It refers to a brief summary or overview of the main points or plot of a story, book, movie, or other narrative.

首都クリスタルへのモンゴール軍の奇襲により、中原の歴史ある国パロは滅亡の危機に瀕していた。国王、王妃までもがモンゴール兵の手により殺害されるという状況の中、家臣はパロ王家に太古より伝わる古代機械（物質転送装置）を用いて、国王の長男にして王太子であるレムスと、その双子の姉リンダを友邦国アルゴスへ移送しようとした。が、古代機械の座標設定に狂いが生じ、2人はあろうことか敵勢力のまっただ中、モンゴール辺境にある魑魅魍魎の跋扈するルードの森へと転送されてしまった。

Amidst a surprise attack by the Mongol army on the capital city of Crystal, the ancient and historic kingdom of Paro faced imminent destruction. In this situation, where even the king and queen were killed by the Mongolian soldiers, the vassals of the Paro royal family attempted to use an ancient machine (a matter transfer device) that had been passed down since ancient times to transport the eldest son of the king and crown prince, Remus, along with his twin sister, Linda, to the friendly neighboring country of Argos. However, due to a malfunction in the coordinates set on the ancient machine, the two found themselves unexpectedly transported right into the heart of enemy territory, the lawless forest of Rood located on the Mongol frontier, infested with all sorts of dangerous creatures.

身を守るすべとてなく、ただ怯えて身を隠すしかなかったレムスとリンダを、ついにモンゴール軍の小隊が発見し、絶体絶命の危機に追いつめる。しかしその時、突如として現われた豹頭人身の異形の超戦士が小隊を全滅させ、双子は難を逃れる。自分自身の名と「アウラ」「ランドック」という2語を除き、全ての記憶を失っていたこの豹頭の男グインは、戦いの後で憔悴し切って倒れるが、リンダの介護によって間もなく体力を取り戻し、以後2人と行動をともにすることとなる。

With no means to defend themselves, Remus and Linda had no choice but to cower in fear and hide. Eventually, a Mongol army squad discovered them, pushing them to the brink of despair. However, at that moment, a monstrous warrior with the head of a leopard suddenly appeared and annihilated the squad, saving the twins from certain death. This leopard-headed man, known only by his name "Guin" and the words "Aura" and "Rundock" that he remembers, had lost all of his other memories. After the battle, Guin collapsed from exhaustion, but with Linda's care, he quickly regained his strength and would henceforth accompany the twins in their journey.

死霊を始めとする魑魅魍魎の襲撃から、一夜の間は辛くも逃れた彼らだったが、翌朝には一帯を支配するモンゴールの出城・スタフォロス城の軍勢に再び発見され、衆寡敵せず投降を余儀なくされる。全身を業病に冒された「黒伯爵」こと城主ヴァーノン伯爵により投獄されたグインらは、隣の牢に収監されていた“紅の傭兵”の異名を持つ若き傭兵・イシュトヴァーンと出逢う。その夜、半獣半人の蛮族セム族がスタフォロス城に攻め入った混乱に乗じ、獄中でリンダと知り合ったセム族の娘スニを加えた4人は、スタフォロス城から眼下に流れる暗黒の河・ケス河へと身を投じて脱出に成功する。そして翌朝、彼らより先に脱出に成功していたイシュトヴァーンが一行に加わり、川の対岸・妖しい瘴気渦巻く砂漠の地ノスフェラスを最初の舞台として、レムスとリンダの、故国を目指す苦難の旅が始まるのである。（以上、第2巻『荒野の戦士』冒頭部まで）

After narrowly escaping the relentless attacks of various creatures, including undead, throughout the night, Remus and Linda were once again discovered by the forces of Stapholos Castle, the outpost controlled by the Mongol army, the next morning. Overwhelmed and outnumbered, they were forced to surrender. Imprisoned by the enigmatic Count Vernon, also known as the "Black Count," who suffered from a debilitating disease, Guin and the others found themselves locked away in a neighboring cell alongside Ishutovar, a young mercenary known as the "Crimson Mercenary."

Taking advantage of the chaos caused by a night-time attack by the Sem tribe, a half-human, half-animal barbarian clan, on Stapholos Castle, Guin and his companions managed to escape by leaping into the dark river, Kesha, flowing below the castle. They successfully made their way to the other side, accompanied by Snah, a Sem tribe girl whom Linda befriended while in captivity.

The following morning, they were joined by Ishutovar, who had successfully escaped before them. Their journey to their homeland, filled with hardships, began in the eerie and mist-covered desert land of Nosferas, across the river. This marked the first stage of Remus and Linda's arduous quest as they aimed to reach their home country (excerpt ends at the beginning of Volume 2, "Warrior of the Wilderness").
この物語の主人公は、タイトルが示す通りグインに他ならないが、物語はグインを含めた群像劇の要素が強く、それゆえ常にグインを中心に語られるわけではない。したがって、20巻以上にわたってグインが不在のままに物語が進行する場合もあり、その時には、他に主役級とされる登場人物や、中原の三大国をはじめとした諸国の宮廷、ときには庶民や脇役の視点までをも交えながら物語が展開することになる。

Indeed, the protagonist of this story is none other than Guin, as the title suggests. However, the story has a strong ensemble element, with a focus on various characters, including Guin. As a result, the narrative doesn't always revolve exclusively around Guin. There are instances where the story progresses for more than 20 volumes without Guin's presence. During those times, other characters designated as main characters, as well as the courts of the three major nations in the central plains and even perspectives of commoners or supporting characters, are incorporated into the narrative as it unfolds.
物語中、歴史はよく運命神ヤーンの織るタペストリーに例えられる。ヤーンは、物語のカギを握る人物を糸とし、彼らをたぐり寄せ、絡ませあうことによって、愛憎や因縁を生みながら、大きな運命の模様を描いていくのである。

In the story, history is often likened to a tapestry woven by the fate god, Yarn. Yarn uses key individuals, who hold the key to the story, as threads, bringing them together and intertwining them to create a tapestry of love, hatred, and destiny. Through these connections and the emotions they generate, Yarn weaves a grand pattern of fate.

『豹頭王の試練』（ひょうとうおうのしれん）は、栗本薫のヒロイック・ファンタジーシリーズ『グイン・サーガ』の記念すべき第100巻。

文庫書下ろしとして発表され、2005年4月15日に、ハヤカワ文庫JAより〈JA789〉として刊行された（ISBN 4-15-030789-X）。また、シリーズ100巻達成を記念して、装丁の異なる特装判が、部数限定で発行され、主にイベント『百の大典』にて、サイン本として販売された。表紙、口絵、挿画はいずれも 丹野忍。

"The Trial of the Leopard-Headed King" (Hyōtō-ō no Shiren) is the momentous 100th volume of Kaoru Kurimoto's heroic fantasy series, Guin Saga.

Released as an original story in paperback format, it was published on April 15, 2005, by Hayakawa Publishing under the imprint Hayakawa Bunko JA (ISBN 4-15-030789-X). To commemorate the achievement of 100 volumes in the series, a limited edition special format with a different cover design was also produced. These special editions were mainly sold as signed copies at the "Hyaku no Taiken" (Festival of a Hundred Chapters) event. The cover illustration, black-and-white illustrations, and inserts were all done by Shinobu Tanno.
